# Dr. Jens Richter: full profile

> A Physicist in Mind, Developer by Heart, Engineer by Passion.

Dr. Jens Richter (Dr.-Ing.) is a trained physicist (Diplom-Physiker), born in
Dresden, raised in Leverkusen, and living in Eschweiler near Aachen with his
wife, son and dog. He is CEO of DNA Evolutions and a hands-on engineer. His
through-line: turning hard, chaotic real-world problems into feasible, optimal
solutions, first in physics, now in logistics software.

## Career arc
Physicist by training, engineer by where it led. In Aachen he studied physics and
computer science, earned a PhD at RWTH Aachen (2018), then moved into software
engineering. At DNA Evolutions
he rose Senior Java Software Engineer (2017) → Partner (2020) → Co-CEO (2024) →
CEO (2026), and still writes the Java. He frames his own career as a constrained
optimization problem: maximize interesting problems, subject to shipping
something real.

## Work: DNA Evolutions GmbH (CEO)
Jens leads DNA Evolutions and still builds **JOpt**, a route and tour
optimization engine written in Java. JOpt takes real-world logistics constraints
(vehicles, drivers, time windows, skills/qualifications, hard constraints, and
multi-day planning) and produces feasible, optimal routes. It is delivered
natively in Java and as Spring WebFlux REST services.

Keywords: vehicle routing problem, operations research, route optimization,
constraint solving, Java, Spring WebFlux, REST APIs.

## Research: PhD in silicon photonics
- Degree: PhD (Dr.-Ing. in Electrical Engineering), RWTH Aachen University, 2018
  (doctoral work 2011–2017).
- Thesis: "Ultra-high-Q inverted silica microtoroid resonators monolithically
  integrated into a silicon photonics platform."
- Official record: https://publications.rwth-aachen.de/record/749969
- In plain terms: he fabricated tiny glass ring resonators on a silicon chip
  that trap light and let it circulate millions of times. That's the basis for very
  precise optical measurements and for optical frequency combs.
- Topics: silicon photonics, ultra-high-Q microresonators, optical frequency
  combs, optical characterization.
- Also: a mild quantum-computing interest ("Quantum Programming 101", D-Wave).

## Education
- Dr.-Ing., Electrical Engineering, RWTH Aachen (2018)
- Diplom, Physics, RWTH Aachen

## Projects (all personal, besides the day job)
- **toPPT** (archived): a MATLAB → PowerPoint generator that turns MATLAB figures
  and results directly into PowerPoint slides. ~6.7k downloads on MATLAB File
  Exchange, 5.0-star rating. Repo: https://github.com/jrichter24/toPPT ·
  File Exchange: https://de.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/fileexchange/44851-jrichter24-toppt
- **jonas-barney.com**: a self-published children's coloring book featuring his
  son Jonas and his dog Barney. https://www.jonas-barney.com/
- **JonasBarneyPaint**: a browser paint studio. Color the Jonas & Barney pages on
  screen, or paint anything (not kids-only). https://paint.jonas-barney.com/ ·
  source: https://github.com/jrichter24/miniPaint
- **moldqueen**: control building-block RC toys through one clean API.
  https://jrichter24.github.io/moldqueen/ · repo: https://github.com/jrichter24/moldqueen
- **jrichter24.github.io**: this site's own source. Open source, including the
  Claude Code agents and skills that built it.
  https://github.com/jrichter24/jrichter24.github.io

## Misc.
A bilingual (English + German) section for essays, tutorials and case studies on
optimization, photonics and engineering.
- English index: https://jrichter24.github.io/en/misc/
- German index: https://jrichter24.github.io/de/misc/

## Site colophon
Built with Next.js (static export), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4 and next-intl.
Bilingual (de, en). Design: square corners only, no blur shadows, a four-color
palette (white, black, one blue for work, one red for personal projects).
